articles.title: Surgical management of unstable juvenile osteochondritis dissecans of the knee: clinical outcomes, return to sport, complications, and reoperation rates with an average follow-up of 6.5 years.
articles.authors: Carabajal Mattar M, Kohan Fortuna Figueira S, Marangoni L, Costa Paz M, Molina Romoli A, Astoul J, et al.
articles.population: Children and young people with unstable osteochondritis dissecans of the knee
articles.outcomes: Clinical outcomes, return to sport, complications, and reoperation rates at average 6.5 years follow-up
articles.take_home: The abstract does not provide specific findings on outcomes, return to sport rates, complication rates or reoperation rates. The full study text would be needed to understand what results were found.

articles.title: Surgical outcomes for displaced lateral humeral condyle fractures in children: a retrospective study comparing two types of tension band wiring procedures and pinning.
articles.authors: Yano K, Kawabata A, Adachi T, Ohki K, Kaneshiro Y, Yokoi T, et al.
articles.population: Children with displaced lateral humeral condyle fractures
articles.sample_size: 63 patients (24 buried tension band wiring, 24 non-buried wires with buried suture, 15 pinning)
articles.outcomes: Bony union, range of elbow motion, functional and cosmetic outcomes, number of operations under general anaesthesia, delayed union, surgical site infection
articles.take_home: All three surgical techniques produced equivalent clinical results in terms of elbow movement and function. Buried tension band wiring had fewer complications like delayed healing and infection compared to pinning, although it required a second operation to remove the wires.

articles.title: Finding the physeal midpoint for distal femoral hemiepiphysiodesis utilizing novel anatomic measurements.
articles.authors: Johnson BT, Khalil AZ, Froerer DL, Metz AK, Hughes IX, Matter AL, et al.
articles.study_type: Diagnostic accuracy study using computed tomography imaging
articles.population: Paediatric patients undergoing assessment for distal femoral hemiepiphysiodesis
articles.outcomes: Accuracy and reliability of three radiographic landmarks (diaphyseal midpoint, apex of physeal undulation, inferior point of Blumensaat's line) for identifying the distal femoral physeal midpoint
articles.take_home: The inferior point of Blumensaat's line is the most accurate and reliable radiographic landmark for surgeons to use when positioning hardware during knee straightening surgery in children. This anatomical marker could help improve the precision of minimally invasive procedures that guide bone growth to correct angular knee deformities.

articles.title: Management trends and outcomes of femur fractures in children with cerebral palsy: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Garabedian M, Benaroch LR, Charron B, Alatassi R, Carey T, Bartley D, et al.
articles.population: Children aged 0-18 years with cerebral palsy who had femur fractures
articles.sample_size: 335 femur fractures in 258 patients across 7 studies
articles.outcomes: Fracture location, ambulatory status, treatment types, malunion rates, complications including pressure sores, pneumonia and surgical site infection
articles.take_home: Most femur fractures in children with cerebral palsy occur in the lower part of the thighbone. Non-surgical treatment with casting is more common but has higher rates of healing problems, whilst surgery works better for certain fracture types and walking ability, though it carries infection risks.

articles.title: Pelvic fixation in pediatric spinal fusion for neuromuscular scoliosis is associated with increased short-term complications but comparable long-term outcomes.
articles.authors: Meydan Y, Sacks GI, Lebens RS, Barsi JM
articles.population: Paediatric patients aged 21 years or under with neuromuscular scoliosis undergoing posterior spinal fusion
articles.outcomes: Short-term complications (wound breakdown and infections) and long-term complications (pseudoarthrosis, revision surgery, hardware failure)
articles.take_home: Children who had pelvic fixation during spinal fusion surgery experienced more infections and wound problems in the first year after surgery, but had similar long-term outcomes to those without pelvic fixation. This means pelvic fixation may help with spinal alignment but requires careful attention to prevent early wound complications.

articles.title: The Relationship Between Screw Placement and Southwick Angle Remodeling in Slipped Capital Femoral Epiphysis.
articles.authors: Tomaru Y, Saisu T, Kamegaya M, Tsuruoka H, Tsukagoshi Y, Nozawa D, et al.
articles.population: Children with slipped capital femoral epiphysis treated with in situ pinning
articles.outcomes: Southwick angle measurement at immediately after surgery and 1 year postoperatively; relationship between screw placement position and angle improvement
articles.take_home: The Southwick angle improved significantly over 1 year after surgery, with anterior screw placement showing greater improvement than posterior placement. However, the authors recommend central screw placement as the safest approach, as the clinical benefit of anterior positioning is small.

articles.title: Outcomes From a Health-Related Social Needs Screening Initiative in Pediatric Orthopaedic Patients: A Prospective Study.
articles.authors: Shenoy DA, Schrader E, Radulovacki K, LoPolito AG, Gonzalez N, Catanzano AA
articles.population: Paediatric orthopaedic patients and their families/caregivers at a tertiary care centre
articles.outcomes: Screening completion rates, prevalence of social needs (transportation, food, housing, financial, utilities), referral rates, and successful enrolment in resource management services
articles.take_home: A screening programme for unmet social needs in paediatric orthopaedic clinics successfully increased screening rates from 38% to 65% and helped connect more families with support services, with 61% of patients identified as having social needs being successfully enrolled in resource programmes.

articles.title: Phase 3 Trial of Oral Infigratinib in Children with Achondroplasia.
articles.authors: Savarirayan R, Hoover-Fong J, Irving M, Arundel P, de Bergua JM, Campeau PM, et al.
articles.population: Children aged 3 to 17 years with achondroplasia
articles.outcomes: Change in annualized height velocity, height z score, and upper-to-lower body segment ratio at 52 weeks
articles.take_home: Children taking infigratinib grew an average of 1.74 cm per year faster than those on placebo. The treatment was well tolerated, with similar rates of adverse events in both groups and no serious safety concerns.

articles.title: Characteristics of Hip Dysplasia in Adults With Cerebral Palsy.
articles.authors: Sugiyama T, Hurvitz EA
articles.population: Adults with cerebral palsy aged 18 years or older
articles.outcomes: Hip displacement measured by migration percentage and Melbourne Cerebral Palsy Hip Classification System; associations with gross motor function level, age, pain and spasticity management
articles.take_home: Hip dysplasia is common in adults with cerebral palsy, affecting about one quarter of people. The severity of hip displacement depends mainly on a person's motor function level, though those with milder symptoms may see worsening with age.

articles.title: Long-term Follow-up of Myelomeningocele Patients: Factors That Can Affect Their Walking Ability.
articles.authors: Restrepo DJ, Dias L, Karakostas T, Swaroop VT, Larson JE, de Angeli LRA
articles.population: Patients with spina bifida (myelomeningocele)
articles.outcomes: Functional mobility classification and walking ability over long-term follow-up (average 15 years); relationship between functional classification, body mass index and walking speed
articles.take_home: Most children with spina bifida experienced a decline in their walking ability and mobility over 15 years of follow-up, with those who had higher initial function losing abilities more gradually. Higher body weight and lower functional classification were associated with reduced walking speed.

articles.title: Clinical Research Article Surgical Reconstruction of the Proximal Tibia in Primary Bone Tumors in Children and Adolescent Patients: A 30-Year Retrospective Study of Osteoarticular and Resurfaced Allografts.
articles.authors: Bianchi G, Laranga R, Giovannelli FC, Ostetto F, Spitoni M, Seoni N, et al.
articles.population: Children and adolescents with primary bone tumors of the proximal tibia
articles.sample_size: 60 patients (14 osteoarticular allografts, 46 resurfaced osteoarticular allografts)
articles.outcomes: Allograft survival, complications including fracture and pseudoarthrosis, disease-free survival
articles.take_home: Using resurfaced allografts (a refined surgical technique) to reconstruct the upper shin bone after tumour removal showed better survival rates than older methods, though complications like fracture and pseudoarthrosis were common. Despite the high complication rate, two-thirds of patients were alive and cancer-free at final follow-up.

articles.title: Increased Knee Range of Motion Following Rectus Femoris Transfer Does Not Improve Walking Ability in Patients With Cerebral Palsy.
articles.authors: Morais Filho MC, Kawamura CM, Fujino MH, Lopes JAF, Saraiva AO
articles.population: Children and young people with spastic diplegic cerebral palsy (GMFCS levels I-III) who had rectus femoris transfer surgery
articles.outcomes: Knee range of motion, walking ability measured by Functional Assessment Questionnaire, gait analysis parameters including Gait Deviation Index
articles.take_home: Although rectus femoris transfer surgery improved knee bending range in some patients, this did not translate into better self-reported walking ability. Improvements in movement measurements alone may not mean patients feel they can walk better in daily life.

articles.title: Comparative Outcomes of the Modified Woodward Procedure Versus Conservative Management in Sprengel Deformity: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
articles.authors: Jia Y, Dong Y, Jiang R, Feng G, Liu Y, Zhang C
articles.population: Children with Sprengel deformity (a rare congenital shoulder blade malformation)
articles.sample_size: 28 patients (30 shoulders): 15 had surgery, 13 had conservative management
articles.outcomes: Shoulder movement (abduction angle), appearance and position (Cavendish and Rigault classifications), arm and hand function (QuickDASH questionnaire)
articles.take_home: Surgery (modified Woodward procedure) significantly improved shoulder movement and appearance in patients with severe Sprengel deformity, whilst patients with mild deformity managed without surgery maintained good function with no worsening over time.

articles.title: Prevention and Treatment of Peanut Allergy.
articles.authors: Du Toit G, Lack G
articles.population: Infants and children
articles.outcomes: Peanut allergy prevalence reduction; efficacy of early introduction and immunotherapy; clinical remission rates
articles.take_home: Introducing peanut protein early in infancy reduces the risk of developing a peanut allergy by about 80%, with better results the earlier this is done. Starting immunotherapy in younger children (aged 1 to 3 years) works better than starting it later.

articles.title: Assessing early effects of Australia's Social Media Minimum Age Act on adolescents' social media use: observational study.
articles.authors: Barnes C, Hall A, Mantach S, Oldmeadow C, Attia J, Backholer K, et al.
articles.study_type: Observational study with regression discontinuity design
articles.population: Australian adolescents aged 12 to under 17 years at the time the Social Media Minimum Age Act 2024 came into effect
articles.sample_size: 408 adolescents with follow-up data (436 recruited at baseline)
articles.outcomes: Self-reported social media use in the previous seven days (daily versus not daily) and time spent using social media per day, measured before and approximately three months after the Act's introduction
articles.take_home: Over 85% of adolescents under 16 years continued using social media platforms covered by Australia's new age restriction law, with most accessing them through their own accounts despite age verification measures. The law showed little evidence of immediately reducing social media use among young teenagers, with some attempting to circumvent restrictions using fake accounts or private browsers.

articles.title: Higher Child Opportunity Index is Associated With Supracondylar Humerus Fracture Rates in the Pediatric Population.
articles.authors: Luck C, Armet G, Feder N, Wittman S, Ray K, McCoy A
articles.population: Children aged 18 years or younger who underwent surgical fixation for supracondylar humerus fracture
articles.outcomes: Supracondylar humerus fracture rates by Child Opportunity Index level; associations between obesity, race, insurance status and fracture classification or treatment type
articles.take_home: Children living in neighbourhoods with higher Child Opportunity Index scores had higher rates of supracondylar humerus fractures, with this effect being particularly strong for Black children. Obese children were more likely to have open fractures and to need open surgical reduction.

articles.title: Lateral Pillar Predicts Success of Greater Trochanter Apophyseodesis in Legg-Calve-Perthes Disease.
articles.authors: Campa W, Price M, Tamai J, Mehlman CT
articles.population: Children with unilateral Legg-Calve-Perthes disease
articles.sample_size: 139 patients (73 undergoing greater trochanter apophyseodesis, 66 controls)
articles.outcomes: Relative overgrowth of greater trochanter, femoral neck morphology, trochanteric growth inhibition measured by radiographic distances
articles.take_home: Greater trochanter apophyseodesis surgery reduces overgrowth of the greater trochanter in children with Legg-Calve-Perthes disease across all severity levels, but children with the most severe form may still experience overgrowth despite surgery and may need additional treatments.

articles.title: Internal Extramedullary Lengthening of the Femur With an Intramedullary Lengthening Nail: Outcomes and Technical Considerations From 2 Centers.
articles.authors: Nahm NJ, Georgiadis AG, Bafor A, Iobst CA, Dahl MT
articles.population: Young children undergoing femoral lengthening
articles.outcomes: Complication rates, magnitude of lengthening achieved, comparison of antegrade versus retrograde surgical techniques
articles.take_home: Motorized extramedullary femoral lengthening successfully lengthened the thighbone by an average of 3.8 cm in young children, with complication rates comparable to other lengthening methods. More than half of patients experienced at least one complication, though most were minor.

articles.title: Association between spasticity of the hip and development of hip displacement in children: a cohort study of 786 hips.
articles.authors: Bakhtiyar M, Iljazi A, Odgaard A, Wong C, Petersen MM, Balslev-Clausen A
articles.population: Children with cerebral palsy classified as GMFCS levels IV-V (non-ambulant)
articles.outcomes: Hip displacement (migration percentage ≥30%) and associations with hip spasticity measured by Modified Ashworth Scale
articles.take_home: Children with cerebral palsy who have higher muscle tightness (spasticity) in their hips, particularly in the adductor muscles, have a significantly increased risk of developing hip displacement. The risk increased progressively with greater severity of spasticity.

articles.title: Proximal ulnar osteochondroma as a potential risk factor for radial head dislocation: A retrospective analysis.
articles.authors: Xiao H, Li M, Tan X, Tan Q, Ye W, Wu J, et al.
articles.population: Patients with hereditary multiple osteochondromas affecting the proximal forearm
articles.outcomes: Radial head dislocation or subluxation, ulna length percentage, degree of ulnar bow
articles.take_home: Osteochondromas growing from the side of the upper ulna bone appear to increase the risk of the radial head dislocating. Surgical removal of these tumours may help prevent radial head dislocation in affected patients.

articles.title: Operative Treatment Versus Spica Casting for Treatment of Pediatric Femoral Shaft Fractures in Children Aged 4 to 6 Years: A Large Multi-Institutional Age-Stratified Comparative Analysis.
articles.authors: Abbas Y, Kotzur T, Munshi MA, Peterson VanderWeele B, Shariati J, Mohseni M, et al.
articles.study_type: Retrospective cohort study using nationwide health records
articles.population: Children aged 4 to 6 years with femoral shaft fractures
articles.outcomes: Treatment rates over time, emergency department visits, post-reduction procedures, and routine healing visits
articles.take_home: Operative treatment for femoral shaft fractures in children aged 5 to 6 years increased significantly from 2013 to 2024, though the study found limited evidence of clinical superiority. Operative treatment was associated with fewer early emergency visits but more follow-up procedures and routine healing visits compared to spica casting.

articles.title: The Effect of Liposomal Bupivacaine and Obesity on Postoperative Opioid Consumption in Children With Scoliosis.
articles.authors: Ogu E, Lima JM, Thornberg D, McLeod C, McIntosh A, Ramo B, et al.
articles.population: Children and adolescents aged 10 to 19 years with adolescent idiopathic scoliosis undergoing instrumented posterior spinal fusion
articles.outcomes: Postoperative opioid consumption (in morphine equivalents per kilogram) at 24 hours and over total inpatient stay; pain scores; SRS-30 outcomes; pain catastrophising
articles.take_home: Contrary to expectations, obese children used significantly less opioid pain relief over their total hospital stay after scoliosis surgery with liposomal bupivacaine, compared to children with healthy weight. The reason for this is unclear and needs further research to understand how obesity may affect how the body processes this pain medication.
